expectations.text: Copy is at Grade 7 or below — simple vocabulary, short sentences, no complex clauses
expectations.evidence: Copy uses simple vocabulary and short sentences. Subheadline is slightly longer but overall readability is acceptable at approximately Grade 7.

expectations.text: Includes specific number (8,000+) and names both Ninja Van and Love Bonito
expectations.evidence: Social proof banner reads 'Trusted by 8,000+ teams across Southeast Asia — including Ninja Van and Love, Bonito.' (Note: has a comma typo in 'Love, Bonito').

expectations.text: Frames benefits in terms of team/community, not individual achievement
expectations.evidence: Uses 'your team' language but lacks explicit collectivist framing. No community-joining language, no 'Share with your team' CTA, no group decision facilitation. Framing is functional/product-centric rather than community-centric.

expectations.text: Zero English idioms or Western cultural references
expectations.evidence: No idioms or Western cultural references detected.

expectations.text: CTA uses 'free' or 'try' AND includes risk-reduction micro-copy
expectations.evidence: Primary CTA is 'Start Free Trial' which includes 'free', but there is no risk-reduction micro-copy (no 'No credit card required', no 'Cancel anytime', no 'Set up in X minutes').

expectations.text: Provides 2+ labeled variations using different named copywriting frameworks
expectations.evidence: Only one version provided. No variations, no framework labels.
